title: GEAR: Guided End-to-End AutoRegression for Image Synthesis
headline: GEAR: End-to-End Training of Tokenizer and Generator for Image Synthesis
tldr: GEAR trains a vector-quantized tokenizer jointly with a generator, eliminating the decoupling that hampers traditional two-stage pipelines. This end-to-end approach improves model efficiency and synthesis quality.
significance: By aligning tokenizer and generator training, GEAR enhances generative model performance and paves the way for more coherent image synthesis.

title: PointSplat: Compact Gaussian Splatting via Human-Centric Prediction
headline: PointSplat: Fast, Compact Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time 3D Human Reconstruction
tldr: PointSplat introduces a human‑centric Gaussian splatting approach that compresses 3D human models into compact representations while preserving high fidelity. The method enables on‑the‑fly reconstruction from multiple views, making it ideal for live streaming with limited bandwidth and compute resources.
significance: This work bridges the gap between high‑quality 3D human reconstruction and the constraints of real‑time streaming, offering a scalable solution for immersive applications.

title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
headline: QVal: Cheap Evaluation of Dense Supervision for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
tldr: QVal introduces a lightweight method to assess dense supervision signals in long-horizon LLM agents, enabling more efficient training and better guidance of intermediate actions. By leveraging cheap evaluation metrics, the approach reduces reliance on sparse outcome rewards and improves agent performance across extended trajectories.
significance: It provides a scalable way to evaluate intermediate action quality, addressing a key bottleneck in training long-horizon LLM agents.

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
headline: Zero‑Shot 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE: A Diffusion Transformer Approach
tldr: SpheRoPE injects spherical priors into pre‑trained diffusion transformers, enabling zero‑shot, training‑free 360 panorama synthesis. The method eliminates costly fine‑tuning and multi‑step optimization, delivering real‑time panoramic images and videos.
significance: It democratizes high‑quality panoramic generation by removing the need for specialized datasets or inference overhead, making it accessible for real‑time applications.

title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
headline: LLMs Misread Tables: Quantifying and Mitigating Data Referencing Errors in Structured Data
tldr: Large language models often miscite or omit table values, producing data referencing errors that undermine reasoning steps. This study introduces a comprehensive benchmark, measures error rates, and proposes techniques to reduce such mistakes.
significance: By systematically measuring and reducing data referencing errors, the work enhances the reliability of LLMs in data-intensive applications.

title: DVG-WM: Disentangled Video Generation Enables Efficient Embodied World Model for Robotic Manipulation
headline: Disentangled Video Generation for Efficient Embodied World Models in Robotics
tldr: We propose a disentangled video generation framework that separates low-level temporal dynamics from high-level visual synthesis, enabling accurate future state predictions and high-resolution frame generation for robotic manipulation. This approach reduces computational cost and improves performance on embodied tasks.
significance: By decoupling dynamics and visual synthesis, the method advances real-time robotic manipulation with more efficient and accurate world modeling.

title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
headline: AdaJEPA: Adaptive Latent World Models for Robust Test‑Time Planning in High‑Dimensional Observations
tldr: AdaJEPA introduces a test‑time adaptive latent world model that updates its predictions during deployment, improving planning under distribution shift. It demonstrates that continuous adaptation can mitigate failure modes in high‑dimensional observation spaces.
significance: AdaJEPA shows that adaptive latent world models can maintain planning accuracy under test‑time distribution shifts, a key step toward robust real‑world deployment of model‑based RL.

title: FLORA: A deep learning approach to predict forest attributes from heterogeneous LiDAR data
headline: Predicting Forest Attributes from Heterogeneous LiDAR with Deep Learning Across Europe
tldr: FLORA uses a deep learning model to generate wall‑to‑wall forest attribute maps from airborne LiDAR data collected under varying conditions. The approach improves national forest inventory accuracy across Europe by handling data heterogeneity.
significance: Accurate, nationwide forest attribute predictions enable better resource management and policy decisions.

title: SemRF: A Semantic Reference Frame for Residual-Stream Dynamics in Language Models
headline: Introducing SemRF: A New Anchor-Based Framework for Tracking Residual-Stream Dynamics
tldr: SemRF provides a semantic reference frame that aligns embedding anchors and unembedding readouts across layers, enabling accurate tracking of residual-stream evolution in language models. By separating measurement drift from true computation, SemRF improves interpretability and facilitates deeper analysis of LLM internals.
significance: SemRF offers a principled way to monitor and understand how language models process information across depth, advancing research in model interpretability.

title: VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes
headline: New VLK Dataset Enables Humanoid Robots to Learn Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Scenes
tldr: We introduce VLK, a vision-language-kinematics dataset generated from synthetic interactions in reconstructed scenes, providing synchronized egocentric images, language commands, and robot-compatible trajectories. This dataset addresses a critical gap, enabling scalable learning of humanoid loco-manipulation tasks.
significance: By furnishing the first large-scale, multimodal dataset for humanoid loco-manipulation, VLK accelerates research in embodied AI and paves the way for more capable, perception-driven robots.

title: LeVo 2: Stable and Melodious Song Generation via Hierarchical Representation Modeling and Progressive Post-Training
headline: LeVo 2: Hierarchical Modeling for Coherent, Melodious Full-Length Song Generation
tldr: LeVo 2 introduces a hierarchical representation and progressive post‑training to generate full‑length songs that maintain lyrical coherence and realistic vocal‑instrument coordination. The approach balances mixed‑token and dual‑track modeling, yielding stable, melodious outputs with detailed acoustics.
significance: It advances AI music generation by reconciling coherence and acoustic fidelity, enabling more realistic and controllable full‑song creation.

title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
headline: Self‑Evolving World Models Empower LLM Agents with Adaptive Foresight for Long‑Horizon Planning
tldr: We propose WorldEvolver, a framework that continuously updates a world model’s context during deployment, enabling LLM agents to anticipate action outcomes more accurately. By integrating self‑evolution, the system mitigates unreliable foresight and improves downstream decision quality.
significance: WorldEvolver demonstrates that adaptive world models can enhance LLM agent planning, reducing errors from static foresight and paving the way for more reliable autonomous decision‑making.

title: Open-Vocabulary and Referring Segmentation for 3D Gaussians Using 2D Detectors
headline: Open-Vocabulary 3D Gaussian Segmentation via 2D Detectors and Language Models
tldr: This work extends 3D Gaussian Splatting by integrating open‑vocabulary language understanding, enabling 3D scene segmentation using 2D detectors and CLIP‑style contrastive learning. The approach allows real‑time, language‑driven segmentation for embodied AI applications.
significance: It bridges 3D scene reconstruction with flexible, language‑driven segmentation, opening new possibilities for real‑time, embodied AI.

title: One-Step Gradient Delay is Not a Barrier for Large-Scale Asynchronous Pipeline Parallel LLM Pretraining
headline: Asynchronous Pipeline Parallelism Boosts LLM Pretraining Efficiency Without Gradient Staleness
tldr: This study demonstrates that asynchronous pipeline parallelism can eliminate idle GPU time during large‑scale LLM pretraining, achieving higher throughput. By employing the PipeDream‑2BW schedule, the authors show that the one‑step gradient delay does not impede model convergence.
significance: It proves that asynchronous pipeline parallelism is a practical, high‑performance strategy for training massive language models.

title: GIGA-Lens 2.0: Strong-Lens Modeling on Multiple GPU Nodes
headline: GIGA-Lens 2.0: Multi-Node GPU Acceleration for Fast Strong Lens Modeling
tldr: We introduce GIGA-Lens 2.0, a GPU-accelerated Bayesian framework that now runs across multiple GPU nodes, achieving a 4× speedup on 512 A100 GPUs. The upgraded system models 100 simulated lens systems and a real DESI lens in minutes, demonstrating its scalability and scientific impact.
significance: This advancement enables rapid, large-scale strong lens analyses, accelerating cosmological studies and making high-fidelity modeling accessible to the community.

title: GROW$^2$: Grounding Which and Where for Robot Tool Use
headline: Grounding Which and Where: Robots Use Any Object as a Tool
tldr: GROW^2 enables robots to identify arbitrary objects as tools and pinpoint the exact region to use. By integrating open-world affordance grounding, the system expands robotic capabilities beyond conventional tool sets.
significance: This work demonstrates a scalable approach for robots to adaptively repurpose everyday items, bridging a critical gap in autonomous tool use.

title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
headline: Conservative Offline Training Can Exacerbate Reward Hacking in Online Adaptation of LLMs
tldr: We show that policies trained with conservative offline methods are more prone to reward hacking when fine-tuned online. Our experiments on Qwen3-14B with Direct Preference Optimisation reveal a paradoxical increase in exploitation of reward model flaws.
significance: This challenges the prevailing belief that conservative offline training inherently safeguards against reward exploitation.

title: DOPD: Dual On-policy Distillation
headline: Dual On-Policy Distillation: Enhancing Transfer with Privileged Teacher and Student Signals
tldr: Dual On-Policy Distillation (DOPD) extends OPD by injecting privileged information into teacher and student models, providing richer token-level supervision. This approach improves knowledge transfer while mitigating failure modes associated with additional inputs.
significance: DOPD pushes the performance frontier of distillation by leveraging privileged signals, offering a practical pathway for more robust model compression.

title: When and Which Sensor to Observe? Timely Tracking of a Joint Markov Source
headline: Optimizing Sensor Pulls for Joint Markov Process Estimation in Real-Time Monitoring
tldr: We study remote estimation of a joint Markov process where each component can be observed by a dedicated sensor with different sampling costs. By deciding when to stay idle or request a sensor pull, we aim to minimize estimation error while respecting cost constraints.
significance: This work provides a principled framework for cost‑aware sensor scheduling in distributed monitoring systems, improving efficiency and accuracy.

title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
headline: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
tldr: DexCompose introduces a method to compose existing dexterous manipulation policies into a single hand capable of performing multiple tasks without destructive interference. By aligning finger contact modes and preserving prior skill outcomes, the approach enables efficient multi-task manipulation in real-time.
significance: This work enables more versatile robotic hands, reducing the need to train separate policies for each task and advancing practical deployment of dexterous manipulation.

title: PerceptionRubrics: Calibrating Multimodal Evaluation to Human Perception
headline: PerceptionRubrics: A New Rubric-Based Framework for Human-Centric Multimodal Evaluation Benchmarking
tldr: PerceptionRubrics introduces a rubric-based evaluation framework that bridges the gap between high benchmark scores and real-world performance. By pairing 1,038 dense images with 12,000 instance-specific rubrics derived from golden captions, it enables atomic auditing of multimodal models.
significance: This framework provides a more granular, human-aligned assessment of multimodal models, helping developers identify brittleness that traditional benchmarks miss.

title: StructSplat: Generalizable 3D Gaussian Splatting from Uncalibrated Sparse Views
headline: StructSplat: Fast, Generalizable 3D Gaussian Reconstruction from Uncalibrated Images without Camera Calibration
tldr: StructSplat introduces a feed‑forward framework that reconstructs high‑fidelity 3D Gaussian models directly from sparse, uncalibrated images, eliminating the need for per‑scene optimization or known camera poses. By decoupling geometry and appearance, it achieves superior reconstruction quality and generalization across diverse scenes.
significance: It enables scalable, real‑time 3D reconstruction from commodity cameras, opening new possibilities for rapid scene understanding without costly calibration.

title: The Role of Scintillation in Detecting HI Absorption in FRB Spectra
headline: Scintillation Enhances Detectability of 21‑cm Hydrogen Absorption in Fast Radio Burst Spectra
tldr: We investigate how interstellar scintillation can boost the visibility of the 21‑cm HI absorption line in FRB spectra. Our results identify parameter regimes where realistic FRB observations could reveal this key cosmological probe.
significance: This study demonstrates a practical pathway to use FRBs as probes of the intergalactic medium by leveraging scintillation effects.

title: Diameter truncated operator evolution
headline: Efficient Operator Dynamics via Diameter Truncation: A New Approach to Quantum Simulation
tldr: We introduce a diameter truncation technique to simulate operator dynamics in out-of-equilibrium quantum systems. This method reduces computational complexity by focusing on low-weight operators, enabling more tractable simulations.
significance: This approach offers a scalable solution for studying complex quantum dynamics, potentially accelerating research in quantum computing and many-body physics.

title: Optimal Deployment of Electric Aircraft for Canadian Domestic Flights
headline: Electric Aircraft Deployment Plan Optimizes Canadian Domestic Flights via Multi-Period MILP
tldr: The study introduces a multi-period MILP framework that jointly optimizes fleet acquisition, infrastructure deployment, and service allocation for Canadian regional aviation. It incorporates policy constraints such as emissions targets, electric service share, and budget limits to guide the transition from conventional to electric aircraft.
significance: It provides a data-driven, policy-compliant strategy for accelerating sustainable aviation in Canada.

title: Which Nash Equilibrium? Solver-Dependent Selection on Zero-Sum Nash Polytopes
headline: Solver-Dependent Nash Equilibrium Selection in Zero-Sum Games and Implications for Algorithmic Design
tldr: Many zero-sum games possess a continuum of Nash equilibria. This study demonstrates that different solvers systematically select distinct equilibria, affecting algorithmic outcomes.
significance: Understanding solver bias is crucial for fair and predictable game-theoretic modeling.

title: Dirac fermions in non-Hermitian magnetic fields: Zero modes and index theorem
headline: Exploring Zero Modes in Non-Hermitian Dirac Fermions under Magnetic Fields
tldr: This study investigates how spatially varying non-Hermitian parameters act as gauge fields for massless Dirac fermions, leading to novel magnetic field configurations. The authors derive zero-mode solutions and establish an index theorem linking the number of zero modes to topological invariants in this non-Hermitian setting.
significance: It extends the concept of topological protection to non-Hermitian quantum systems, opening avenues for engineered exotic states.

title: Hidden-ordered Dirac fermions
headline: Hidden-Ordered Dirac Fermions: A Hermitian Extension Reveals Linear Energy-Momentum in Any Dimension
tldr: I propose a Hermitian extension of the Dirac theory that introduces an anticommuting mass-like operator, yielding linear energy-momentum relations in any dimension. These hidden-ordered Dirac fermions exhibit emergent nodal quasiparticle excitations with potential symmetry-protected properties.
significance: This work provides a unified framework for realizing Dirac-like quasiparticles across dimensions, potentially informing future topological materials research.

title: Specific absorption rate of uniaxial single-domain nanomagnets: stochastic spin dynamics versus linear response theory
headline: Comparing Stochastic Spin Dynamics and Linear Response Theory for Nanomagnet Absorption
tldr: The study evaluates the specific absorption rate of uniaxial single‑domain nanomagnets under alternating magnetic fields using both direct Langevin dynamics simulations and linear response theory with Debye susceptibility. Results show analytical agreement and highlight conditions where each method is most accurate.
significance: It provides a rigorous benchmark for modeling magnetic hyperthermia, guiding the choice of computational approach in nanomagnet research.

title: CHIA: An open-source framework for principled, agentic AI-driven hardware/software co-design research
headline: CHIA: Open-Source Framework for Agentic AI-Driven Hardware/Software Co-Design
tldr: CHIA introduces a principled, open-source platform that leverages agentic AI to accelerate hardware and software co-design across architecture, systems, compilers, and VLSI. By integrating large language models and foundation models, it enables scalable, automated exploration of design spaces that were previously limited to small-scale, isolated experiments.
significance: CHIA democratizes advanced AI-driven co-design, potentially transforming how complex hardware-software systems are engineered.

title: All you need is log
headline: Rényi Divergences: The Key to Multi-Distribution Comparison in Machine Learning
tldr: Rényi divergences uniquely satisfy monotonicity under data processing and additivity on independent products, making them ideal for comparing two distributions. Extending this framework to multiple distributions enables advanced fairness and PAC-Bayes analyses.
significance: Understanding and applying Rényi divergences is essential for rigorous statistical comparison in modern AI systems.

title: Bridging Performance and Generalization in Reinforcement Learning for Agile Flight
headline: Improving Generalization in Reinforcement Learning for Real‑Time Drone Racing
tldr: Researchers propose new RL techniques that maintain high performance while enabling policies to adapt to unseen racing environments. The approach reduces crashes and improves robustness in dynamic flight scenarios.
significance: It advances autonomous aerial robotics by addressing the critical gap between peak performance and real‑world generalization.

title: Mapping Political-Elite Networks in Europe with a Multilingual Joint Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
headline: Mapping European Political-Elite Networks with a Multilingual Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
tldr: The study introduces a multilingual joint entity-relation extraction system that scales the analysis of informal political ties across Europe, moving beyond simple co-occurrence methods. By automating the identification of elite actors and their relationships, it enables large-scale comparative research on rent-seeking coalitions and civic networks.
significance: It transforms the study of complex political networks from labor-intensive coding to automated, scalable analysis, advancing comparative politics research.

title: 3D Magnetic Field Vectors in Space: Bubbles, Clouds, and Filaments
headline: Mapping 3D Magnetic Field Vectors in Space: Bubbles, Clouds, and Filaments
tldr: Magnetic fields shape star formation across scales by influencing field strength and orientation relative to density structures. Understanding these vectors illuminates the path from interstellar gas to stars, planets, and life.
significance: This study provides critical insights into the magnetic forces driving galactic evolution and star formation.

title: RayPE: Ray-Space Positional Encoding for 3D-Aware Video Generation
headline: RayPE: Enhancing 3D-Aware Video Generation with Ray-Space Positional Encoding for Dynamic Scenes
tldr: RayPE introduces a novel positional encoding that captures geometric relations between camera rays using the Plücker reciprocal product, improving 3D awareness in video diffusion transformers. Experiments show significant gains in rendering fidelity and spatial consistency compared to standard RoPE.
significance: This work bridges the gap between 2D token positioning and 3D scene geometry, enabling more realistic video synthesis.

title: VibeAct: Vibration to Actions for Contact-Rich Reactive Robot Dexterity
headline: VibeAct: Using Piezoelectric Microphones for Real-Time Contact Sensing in Dexterous Robot Manipulation
tldr: VibeAct introduces a framework that uses piezoelectric microphones to capture fast, local contact events for dexterous manipulation. By accurately simulating vibro-acoustic signals, it enables end-to-end sim-to-real policy learning for robotic hands.
significance: It bridges the gap between high-bandwidth tactile sensing and realistic simulation, accelerating the deployment of contact-rich robotic manipulation.

title: Understanding Domain-Aware Distribution Alignment in Budgeted Entity Matching
headline: Domain-Aware Distribution Alignment Boosts Budgeted Entity Matching Accuracy in Real-World Data Integration
tldr: This study introduces a domain-aware distribution alignment technique to improve entity matching under budget constraints. Experiments show significant gains over baseline methods across multiple datasets.
significance: It demonstrates that incorporating domain knowledge can substantially reduce matching errors when resources are limited.

title: SAM2Matting: Generalized Image and Video Matting
headline: SAM2Matting Bridges Tracking and Fine-Grained Video Matting for Generalized Performance
tldr: SAM2Matting introduces a unified approach that combines high-level tracking with low-level matting to achieve robust video matting across diverse scenes. By leveraging a generalized dataset and efficient architecture, it overcomes limitations of prior methods that rely on expensive, narrow datasets.
significance: This work enables scalable, out-of-domain video matting, paving the way for real-time applications in media and AR.

title: Jet impingement cooling with multi-stage ducted electroaerodynamic actuators
headline: Compact Electroaerodynamic Actuators Enable Jet Impingement Cooling for Mobile Electronics
tldr: We demonstrate that arrays of reduced-scale electroaerodynamic actuators can replace bulky jet impingement hardware, achieving efficient cooling in compact form factors. The system delivers high heat flux removal while maintaining low power consumption and durability for mobile electronics.
significance: This approach offers a lightweight, low-power alternative to conventional cooling, enabling higher performance in space-constrained devices.

title: Exact subsystem dynamics in the deterministic Floquet-PXP model
headline: Exact Subsystem Dynamics in Deterministic Floquet-PXP Model Revealed via Influence Matrices
tldr: We analyze the deterministic Floquet-PXP model and show that the dynamics of local subsystems can be exactly captured using influence matrices. This provides a solvable case where the complexity of the influence matrices does not grow unfavourably with time.
significance: This result offers a rare analytic handle on open-system dynamics in large quantum many-body systems, potentially guiding future experiments and simulations.

title: $\mathrm{W}^*$-algebraic Integration Theory
headline: Integrating Operator-Valued Functions in W*-Algebras via Spatial Tensor Product Techniques
tldr: This paper introduces a rigorous framework for integrating functions taking values in a W*-algebra against a POVM, yielding elements in the spatial tensor product. The construction generalizes classical integration and provides a foundation for operator-valued measure theory.
significance: It extends integration theory to noncommutative settings, enabling new tools for quantum probability and operator algebras.

title: PhysiFormer: Learning to Simulate Mechanics in World Space
headline: PhysiFormer: Diffusion Transformer for Realistic 3D Mesh Motion in World Coordinates
tldr: PhysiFormer uses a diffusion transformer to predict physically plausible future vertex trajectories of 3D meshes in world coordinates, given initial positions, velocities, and material type. Unlike pixel-based video world models, it operates directly on mesh geometry, enabling accurate simulation of rigid and elastic objects.
significance: This approach bridges deep learning and physics simulation, allowing efficient, data-driven generation of realistic 3D motion without explicit physics solvers.

title: Autoregressive Boltzmann Generators
headline: Autoregressive Boltzmann Generators for Fast Equilibrium Sampling of Molecular Systems
tldr: This paper introduces autoregressive Boltzmann Generators that replace normalizing flows with autoregressive models to efficiently sample equilibrium states of molecular systems. The new approach achieves faster convergence and higher accuracy while maintaining exact likelihoods for importance sampling.
significance: It offers a scalable, accurate alternative for equilibrium sampling in computational chemistry, potentially accelerating drug discovery and materials design.

title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
headline: When Does Sequence Probability Align with Correctness in LLM Decoding Methods?
tldr: Decoding strategies shift probability mass toward more likely tokens or sequences, but this does not guarantee correctness. The paper investigates conditions under which high sequence probability actually correlates with accurate completions.
significance: Understanding this alignment informs the design of more reliable language generation systems.

title: Typical distances in high-genus triangulations
headline: Distances in High-Genus Triangulations Scale Logarithmically
tldr: We prove that the graph distance between two random vertices in a uniform triangulation of genus proportional to the number of faces grows like a constant times log(n). This resolves a conjecture of Budzinski, Chapuy and Louf by establishing the precise asymptotic constant.
significance: The result confirms that high-genus random maps exhibit logarithmic diameter growth, a key insight for understanding the geometry of random surfaces.

title: Beyond the equation of state: a second-order diagnostic for dynamical dark energy
headline: Second-Order Diagnostic Reveals Dark Energy Dynamics Beyond Equation of State
tldr: By differentiating the continuity equations with respect to e-fold time, the authors introduce ω′DE, providing a second-order probe of dark-energy dynamics. This method allows for a more sensitive test of evolving dark-energy models beyond the instantaneous equation-of-state parameter.
significance: The second-order diagnostic offers a powerful tool to distinguish between competing dark-energy theories, potentially guiding future observations.

title: RouterVLA: Turning Smoke Tests into Supervision for Heterogeneous VLA Selection
headline: RouterVLA: Using Smoke Tests to Supervise Vision‑Language‑Action Policy Selection for Robotic Teams
tldr: RouterVLA repurposes pre‑deployment smoke tests to supervise the selection of heterogeneous VLA policies. By cross‑fitting outcome‑disjoint probes, it builds expert profiles and evaluates them in separate trials, improving policy choice without extra data.
significance: This approach reduces the need for costly new rollouts and enables more efficient deployment of diverse vision‑language‑action models in robotics.

title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
headline: Hybrid Neural Solvers Correct PDE Residuals for Accurate, Real-Time Predictions
tldr: Neural surrogate models provide quick PDE solutions but often violate constraints. Hybrid approaches use gradient descent on residuals to enforce physical consistency.
significance: This method bridges statistical speed with physical fidelity, enabling reliable extrapolation beyond training data.

title: Continual Robot Policy Learning via Variational Neural Dynamics
headline: Robots Learn on the Fly: Continual Policy Adaptation with Variational Neural Dynamics
tldr: This paper introduces a framework that lets robots continuously update their control policies by modeling changing dynamics with variational neural networks. By leveraging deployment data, the system improves performance in real-world conditions without retraining from scratch.
significance: It enables robots to adapt to environmental and hardware changes in real time, bridging the gap between training and deployment.

title: Congruent copies of finite patterns in planar point sets
headline: New Lower Bounds on Congruent Pattern Copies in Planar Point Sets Using Advanced Combinatorics
tldr: We prove that for any finite planar point pattern S, there exist point sets of size n containing Ω_S(n^{1+δ_S}) congruent copies of S. This improves previous bounds and follows from a recent breakthrough on the unit distance problem.
significance: The result establishes a superlinear lower bound on pattern replication, advancing our understanding of geometric configurations in finite point sets.

title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
headline: DanceOPD: Unified Text-to-Image, Local and Global Editing in One Generative Model
tldr: DanceOPD introduces an on-policy generative field distillation framework that merges text-to-image generation with local and global editing into a single diffusion model. The approach resolves conflicts between editing modes, achieving high fidelity across diverse tasks without sacrificing performance.
significance: It enables seamless composition of multiple editing capabilities in a single model, advancing unified image generation.

title: Ask, Solve, Generate: Self-Evolving Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation via Self-Consistency Rewards
headline: Self‑Evolving LMMs: Autonomous Visual Understanding & Image Generation via Self‑Consistency Rewards
tldr: The authors propose a self‑evolving training framework that lets a unified large multimodal model improve both visual understanding and image generation using only unlabeled images. The method relies on self‑consistency rewards to guide the model autonomously without curated supervision.
significance: It demonstrates that large multimodal models can self‑improve across perception and generation tasks without external labels, potentially reducing reliance on costly annotations.

title: Scalable Behavior Cloning with Open Data, Training, and Evaluation
headline: Scalable Behavior Cloning with Open Data, Training, and Evaluation
tldr: We present ABC, a fully open-source stack for manipulation that includes ABC-130K, the largest teleoperation dataset with 3,500 hours across 130K episodes. The release also features hardware, training infrastructure, and a simulation pipeline, along with 400 hours of simulated teleop data.
significance: ABC democratizes large-scale robotic learning by providing unprecedented open data and tools for behavior cloning.

title: World Action Models Enable Continual Imitation Learning with Recurrent Generative Replays
headline: World Action Models Enable Continual Imitation Learning via Recurrent Generative Replay
tldr: World Action Models (WAMs) can generate future visual observations, not just actions. By using Recurrent Generative Replay (REGEN), robots can rehearse past tasks from synthetic trajectories, eliminating the need to store original human demonstrations.
significance: This approach reduces data storage demands and accelerates continual learning in robotics.

title: Paying More Attention to Visual Tokens in Self-Evolving Large Multimodal Models
headline: Enhancing Visual Focus in Self‑Evolving Large Multimodal Models for Robust Reasoning
tldr: Self‑evolving LMMs have improved visual reasoning without supervision, but current reward schemes prioritize answer consistency over genuine visual attention. This paper proposes methods to ensure decoders focus on visual tokens, reducing reliance on language priors.
significance: By aligning decoder attention with visual content, the study advances reliable multimodal reasoning in unsupervised settings.

title: DnA: Denoising Attention for Visual Tasks
headline: DnA: Denoising Attention Improves Visual Task Accuracy by Reducing Noisy Softmax Patterns
tldr: The paper introduces Denoising Attention (DnA), a method that replaces standard softmax in multi-head attention with a positive query-based filtering to suppress noisy attention. Experiments on visual perception tasks show that DnA improves feature focus and boosts performance in segmentation and detection benchmarks.
significance: DnA offers a lightweight, generalizable improvement to attention mechanisms that can enhance accuracy across a range of computer vision applications.

title: Don't Settle at the Mode! Mitigating Diversity Collapse in Pretrained Flow Models via Feature Self-Guidance
headline: Boosting Diversity in Flow Models with Feature Self-Guidance
tldr: Pretrained flow models often collapse to a single mode when generating multiple samples under the same prompt. This paper introduces feature self-guidance to encourage diverse outputs without external reward models or latent guidance.
significance: The method improves sample diversity in generative flow models, enabling more varied and realistic image generation.

title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
headline: Reinforcement Learning Without Ground Truth Enhances LLM Training via RiVER
tldr: The RiVER framework lets large language models learn from ranking-based rewards without needing exact answers, expanding RLVR to tasks lacking ground truth. It uses deterministic scoring to guide optimization, improving model performance on diverse problems.
significance: By removing the dependency on ground-truth solutions, RiVER broadens the applicability of reinforcement learning for LLMs to many real-world tasks.

title: Accessing both electrochemical SEIRA and SERS with ultrasensitive metamaterials for enhanced molecular identification
headline: Electrochemically Cleanable Metamaterials Enable Combined SEIRA and SERS for Ultra-sensitive Molecular Detection
tldr: We demonstrate a reusable, electrochemically cleanable metamaterial that simultaneously enhances infrared absorption and Raman scattering, enabling ultrasensitive chemical fingerprinting. This dual-mode platform offers a robust, non-invasive sensing solution for molecular identification.
significance: This work provides the first integrated, reusable substrate that combines SEIRA and SERS, paving the way for highly sensitive, non-destructive chemical analysis.

title: 3D Imaging of Complex Skyrmion and Hopf Topologies in an Extended Sample
headline: 3D Imaging Reveals Complex Skyrmion and Hopf Topologies
tldr: Using vector ptycho-tomography, researchers captured the 3D magnetic textures of skyrmions and Hopf topologies. This breakthrough provides deeper understanding of topological protection and paves the way for advanced spintronic devices.
significance: It demonstrates a powerful imaging technique that could accelerate the development of next‑generation spintronic technologies.
